specification.topic-statement: The input buffer cursor tracks the insertion point within a UTF-8 text buffer and moves through the text in response to navigation operations, respecting grapheme cluster boundaries for horizontal movement and preserving column position across vertical movement.

scope.in-scope: Cursor position representation, horizontal and vertical movement, line-boundary navigation, buffer-boundary behavior, target column preservation, and synchronization with the text buffer.
scope.boundary: Text insertion and deletion operations update the cursor position after modifying the buffer. This specification documents the cursor-side effects of those operations, not the editing operations themselves.
The cursor position drives how the input area is displayed. What the terminal renders is out of scope.

field.name: byte offset
field.note: position in UTF-8 bytes from the start of the buffer; ranges from 0 to text length inclusive

field.name: grapheme offset
field.note: position in grapheme clusters from the start of the buffer; synchronized with byte offset

field.name: target column
field.note: the grapheme column to aim for during vertical movement; zero means use the current column

behavior.position-representation:
The cursor position is maintained as two synchronized values: a byte offset and a grapheme offset. The byte offset is the absolute position in the UTF-8 byte sequence. The grapheme offset is the count of grapheme clusters from the start of the buffer to the cursor.
The valid range for the byte offset is zero to the current text length inclusive. A byte offset equal to the text length represents the position after the last character.
When the byte offset is set directly, the grapheme offset is recalculated from scratch by scanning the text from the beginning to the new byte offset, counting grapheme cluster boundaries using Unicode grapheme break rules. The grapheme offset is never set independently.
behavior.horizontal-movement:
Horizontal movement advances or retreats the cursor by exactly one grapheme cluster. Grapheme cluster boundaries are determined by the Unicode grapheme break algorithm, which accounts for combining characters, zero-width joiners, and other multi-codepoint sequences that form a single visible glyph.
When moving left, if the byte offset is zero, the operation is a no-op. Otherwise, the text is scanned from the beginning to identify the grapheme cluster boundary immediately before the current position. The cursor advances to that boundary. The grapheme offset decrements by one.
When moving right, if the byte offset equals the text length, the operation is a no-op. Otherwise, the next grapheme cluster boundary after the current position is identified and the cursor advances to it. The grapheme offset increments by one.
After any horizontal movement, the target column is reset to zero, clearing any preserved column from a previous vertical movement.
behavior.vertical-movement:
Vertical movement places the cursor on the line above or below the current line, at the same column position when possible.
The column for vertical movement is measured in codepoints from the start of the current line, not in grapheme clusters. Each Unicode codepoint contributes one unit to the column count, regardless of whether it forms part of a multi-codepoint grapheme cluster.
Horizontal movement uses true Unicode grapheme cluster boundaries, but vertical movement measures columns by codepoint count. A text containing combining characters or ZWJ sequences will produce different effective positions when arriving at a column via vertical movement versus arriving via horizontal movement.
Before the first vertical move in a sequence, the current column is computed and stored as the target column. This target column is preserved across subsequent vertical movements without intervening horizontal movements or text edits.
When the target line is shorter than the target column, the cursor is placed at the end of the target line. If a subsequent vertical move reaches a longer line, the cursor returns to the target column (not to the clamped position used on the shorter line).
The target column is reset to zero by any horizontal movement, any line-boundary navigation, and any text modification. After a reset, the next vertical movement establishes a new target column from the current cursor position.
When moving up, if the cursor is on the first line (no newline exists before the cursor), the operation is a no-op.
Otherwise, the current line start and current column are determined. The target column is set if not already set. The previous line is identified by scanning backward from the current line start. The cursor is placed at the target column within the previous line, clamped to the line's length.
When moving down, if the cursor is on the last line (no newline exists at or after the cursor), the operation is a no-op.
Otherwise, the current column and target column are determined. The next line is identified by scanning forward past the current newline. The cursor is placed at the target column within the next line, clamped to the line's length.

line-boundary-navigation.move-to-line-start: The cursor moves to the position immediately after the nearest preceding newline, or to position zero if no preceding newline exists. If the cursor is already at the line start, the operation is a no-op. The target column is reset to zero.
line-boundary-navigation.move-to-line-end:
The cursor moves to the position of the nearest following newline character. If no following newline exists, the cursor moves to the text length. If the cursor is already at that position, the operation is a no-op. The target column is reset to zero.
The line-end position is the byte offset of the newline character itself. Pressing right from this position will advance past the newline onto the next line, rather than staying at the current line end.

behavior.cursor-after-text-edits:
Text modification operations update the cursor after changing the buffer.
When a character is inserted at the current position, the cursor advances by the number of bytes the inserted character occupies in UTF-8 encoding. The grapheme offset is recalculated. The target column is reset to zero.
When a character before the cursor is deleted, the cursor retreats by the number of bytes of the deleted character. The entire character (which may be multiple bytes for non-ASCII codepoints) is deleted as a unit. At byte offset zero, deletion is a no-op and the cursor does not move. The target column is reset to zero.
When a character at the cursor position is deleted, the cursor position does not change. At the text end, deletion is a no-op. The target column is reset to zero.
When the entire buffer is cleared, the cursor is reset to byte offset zero and grapheme offset zero. The target column is reset to zero.
behavior.empty-buffer: When the buffer contains no text, every movement operation is a no-op. The cursor remains at byte offset zero and grapheme offset zero. No target column is established.
